Home / Project Management & Kanban / Alternatives to Asana

3 open source alternatives to Asana

Project and task management for teams. Here are the open source projects real teams use instead — ranked by fit, with honest pros and cons for each.

What people don't love about Asana

  • Pricing jumps significantly beyond Basic tier.
  • Steep learning curve for advanced features.
  • {'Lock-in': 'exporting workflows is painful.'}

Current Asana pricing (for reference): Free tier for up to 10; Premium from $10.99/user/month.

Ad slot — above comparison

Quick comparison

Alternative Best for License Self-host Hosted cloud?
Plane
Open source JIRA alternative with cycles and modules. 		Modern teams that want Linear-style sprints and cycles. AGPL-3.0 ★★★☆☆ Yes
OpenProject
Enterprise-ready open source project management. 		Teams that need Gantt, roadmaps and enterprise controls. GPL-3.0 ★★★☆☆ Yes
Taiga
Agile project management with Scrum and Kanban. 		Scrum-first teams who like structured agile flows. Mozilla-2.0 ★★★☆☆ Yes

1. Plane — Modern teams that want Linear-style sprints and cycles.

Open source JIRA alternative with cycles and modules.

Strengths

  • Modern UI inspired by Linear/Jira.
  • Cycles, modules and views.
  • Active community and rapid updates.

Weaknesses

  • Still adding features at pace — occasional rough edges.
  • Self-hosted setup involves several services.
  • Fewer integrations than mature incumbents.
License: AGPL-3.0 Self-host difficulty: 3/5 Hosted cloud option

Plane homepage · Source on GitHub · Asana vs Plane →

2. OpenProject — Teams that need Gantt, roadmaps and enterprise controls.

Enterprise-ready open source project management.

Strengths

  • Strong Gantt, roadmaps and budget features.
  • BIM (Building Information Modeling) for construction industry.
  • Active enterprise adoption.

Weaknesses

  • Community edition lacks some premium features.
  • Setup has more moving parts than lightweight tools.
  • UI density can intimidate newcomers.
License: GPL-3.0 Self-host difficulty: 3/5 Hosted cloud option

OpenProject homepage · Source on GitHub · Asana vs OpenProject →

3. Taiga — Scrum-first teams who like structured agile flows.

Agile project management with Scrum and Kanban.

Strengths

  • Dedicated Scrum and Kanban flows.
  • Clean, focused UI.
  • Free cloud tier available.

Weaknesses

  • Self-hosting is multi-service.
  • Smaller integration ecosystem than Jira.
  • Reporting features are lighter.
License: Mozilla-2.0 Self-host difficulty: 3/5 Hosted cloud option

Taiga homepage · Source on GitHub · Asana vs Taiga →
